name: The Oasis by Emaar
researcher_notes: Total development value was initially AED 34 billion at launch (2023), expanded to approximately AED 73 billion (~USD 20 billion) in early 2024 following masterplan expansion.

Emaar's official page lists approximately 2,600 villas; third-party sources cite up to 7,000 units across all phases — discrepancy likely reflects phased rollout vs. full masterplan count. Use 7,000 as full masterplan figure with caveat.

Palmiera (Phase 1) launched at approximately AED 8.4–8.5 million and secondary market prices reported at AED 13–14 million as of early 2025 per broker sources; these are broker-reported figures, not DLD-verified transaction data.
ROI figures (50–100% return on equity claims) originate from broker marketing materials (Xperience Realty LinkedIn post) and are not independently verified. Treat with caution.
Projected rental yields of 5–7% cited by multiple broker and aggregator sources; no DLD rental index data available to confirm.

Handover dates vary by sub-community: Palmiera ~Q4 2027, Mirage ~Q2 2028, Palmiera 2 ~Q2 2028, Palmiera 3 ~Q4 2028, Lavita ~Q4 2028, Address Villas Tierra ~Q2 2029, Palace Villas Ostra ~Q3 2028–Q3 2029, Mareva ~Q1 2030.
Driving distances sourced from Emaar official website and homeland.ae; minor variations exist across sources. Emaar-stated distances used as primary reference.
No metro station within the community; nearest is Expo 2020 Metro Station (~15 min drive) or Dubai Investment Park Metro Station per Christie's Real Estate.
Schools listed are nearby but not within the community boundary; no on-site school confirmed as open/operational yet.
Mirage The Oasis fact sheet (Apex Capital PDF) confirms 204 total units: 123 five-bedroom and 81 six-bedroom villas.
Construction progress for Mirage reported at 19.22% as of November 2025 per PropertyFinder.
The community is located in Dubailand at the intersection of Sheikh Zayed Bin Hamdan Al Nahyan Street (D54) and Yalayis Street/Jebel Ali-Al Hibab Road (D57).
Lavita is described as a limited collection of 43 ultra-luxury mansions (6–7 bedrooms) per Alkira.ae; prices from AED 36–38 million per multiple sources.
Address Villas – Tierra is a branded collaboration between Emaar and Address Hotels + Resorts.

investment_data.ownership: Freehold — available to UAE residents and foreign nationals
investment_data.golden_visa_threshold: AED 2 million minimum property value (all units exceed this threshold)
investment_data.payment_plan_structure: Typically 80/20 or 90/10 (10% down payment, balance during construction, 10–20% on handover)
investment_data.projected_roi_percent: 5–7% rental yield (broker projections; unverified by DLD data); capital appreciation of 25–35% within 2 years cited by conquer.ae (unverified)
investment_data.palmiera_price_movement: Palmiera Phase 1 launched at ~AED 8.4M; secondary market prices reported at ~AED 13–14M as of early 2025 (broker-reported, not DLD-verified)
investment_data.branded_premium_note: Address Villas – Tierra and Palace Villas – Ostra carry brand premiums associated with Address Hotels + Resorts and Palace Hotel brands respectively
investment_data.capital_appreciation_note: Broker sources cite 50%+ price growth in comparable Dubailand communities (Al Barari, DAMAC Hills) between 2021–2024; not independently verified for The Oasis specifically
investment_data.construction_timelines: 2027–2030 depending on phase; reinforces long-term investment positioning

development_level.headline_stats: 100 million sq ft masterplan | ~7,000 villas & mansions | AED 73 billion total development value | 25% of land dedicated to water and green spaces
development_level.scarcity_argument: Emaar's first ultra-luxury lagoon villa community; low-density design with only ~7,000 units across 100M sq ft — one of the lowest density luxury communities in Dubai. All units are freehold standalone villas or mansions; no apartments.
development_level.lifestyle_pitch: Resort living as a permanent address: swimmable crystal lagoons, 54km cycling network, private beaches, wellness gardens, and clubhouses — all within a gated, low-density sanctuary 18 minutes from Al Maktoum International Airport and 20 minutes from Dubai Hills Mall.
development_level.investment_pitch: Freehold ownership with Golden Visa eligibility from AED 2M (all units qualify). Emaar's track record across Arabian Ranches, Dubai Hills Estate, and Downtown Dubai demonstrates consistent long-term capital appreciation. Palmiera Phase 1 secondary market prices have risen significantly from launch. Projected rental yields of 5–7% for villa segment. Prices remain below comparable ready villas in Emirates Hills or Palm Jumeirah.
development_level.objection_handling: Construction timelines run to 2028–2030, but Emaar has delivered ~108,000 units globally since 2002 with a strong track record. Extended payment plans (80/20 or 90/10) mean buyers spread capital through construction, reducing upfront exposure. The area is car-dependent currently, but proximity to major highways (D54, E611) and planned infrastructure improvements mitigate this. No metro on-site, but Expo 2020 Metro Station is ~15 minutes away.

per_sub_development.palmiera: Phase 1 — the original benchmark. Secondary market already active with significant price appreciation from launch. Ideal for buyers who want the earliest-phase Emaar community asset.
per_sub_development.mirage_the_oasis: 204 units only — 5 & 6-bedroom villas, nearly all water-facing. Three architectural styles. Lift and driver's room in every villa. Q2 2028 handover. Construction underway at 19%.
per_sub_development.palmiera_2: 4-bedroom family villas continuing the Palmiera legacy. Q2 2028 handover. Entry-level pricing within The Oasis for families seeking space and community.
per_sub_development.palmiera_3: Nature-centric 4-bedroom villas near the 54km cycling route. Limited waterfront units. Q4 2028 handover. Active outdoor lifestyle focus.
per_sub_development.lavita: Only 43 mansions. 6–7 bedrooms. 21,000–35,500+ sq ft. Car galleries, private cinemas, lagoonfront plots. The rarest asset in The Oasis.
per_sub_development.address_villas_tierra: Address Hotels + Resorts branded residences. 4–6 bedrooms. Five-star hospitality design standards. Q2 2029 handover. Strong appeal to international branded-residence buyers.
per_sub_development.palace_villas_ostra: Palace-branded designer villas. 4–6 bedrooms. Water-inspired architecture. Lagoon-facing. Q3 2028–2029 handover.
per_sub_development.mareva: 4–6 bedroom villas. Q1 2030 handover. Longest payment runway in the community — suitable for buyers wanting maximum construction-phase payment flexibility.

location.description: Located at the intersection of Sheikh Zayed Bin Hamdan Al Nahyan Street (D54) and Yalayis Street (D57) in Dubailand. A low-density, water-centric luxury villa community spanning ~100 million sq ft. Positioned in southern Dubai with strong highway connectivity but currently car-dependent for daily commuting. Nearest metro is Expo 2020 Metro Station (~15 min drive).

community_amenities: Man-made beaches and private beach access
Planned retail hub (~1.5 million sq ft)

investment.projected_roi_percent: 5–7% rental yield (broker projections; unverified by official DLD data). Capital appreciation of 25–35% within 2 years cited by third-party sources (unverified).
investment.branded_premium_note: Address Villas – Tierra carries Address Hotels + Resorts brand premium. Palace Villas – Ostra carries Palace Hotel brand association. Both command pricing premiums over non-branded phases.

sales_talking_points.headline_stats: 100M sq ft | ~7,000 villas & mansions | AED 73B development value | 25% open space
sales_talking_points.scarcity: Emaar's first ultra-luxury lagoon community; low-density standalone villas only; no apartments
sales_talking_points.lifestyle: Permanent resort living: swimmable lagoons, 54km cycling, private beaches, wellness facilities, gated privacy
sales_talking_points.investment: Freehold + Golden Visa eligible. Emaar delivery track record (~108,000 units). Competitive vs. Emirates Hills and Palm Jumeirah ready villas. 80/20 payment plans spread capital through construction.
sales_talking_points.connectivity: 18 min to Al Maktoum Airport, 20 min to Dubai Hills Mall, 35 min to Downtown Dubai. Major highways D54 and E611 provide direct access across Dubai.
sales_talking_points.family_suitability: Multiple international schools within 15–20 min drive. Large villa plots with private gardens. Kids' play areas, cycling tracks, and community parks within the development.
sales_talking_points.objection_car_dependency: Community is car-dependent; Expo 2020 Metro Station ~15 min drive. RTA road network expansion ongoing. Ride-hailing services readily available.
sales_talking_points.objection_construction: Phased handovers 2027–2030. Emaar has delivered ~108,000 units globally. Construction progress visible and tracked publicly.

prompt_data.developer_track_record: Emaar Properties is Dubai's most prominent master community developer, responsible for Burj Khalifa, Dubai Mall, Downtown Dubai, Dubai Marina, Dubai Hills Estate, and Arabian Ranches. The company reported AED 26.7 billion in revenue for 2023 and has delivered approximately 108,000 residential units globally since 2002. Early investors in Emaar master communities (Downtown Dubai, Dubai Hills Estate, Arabian Ranches) have historically achieved significant capital appreciation.

audit_flags: ROI and capital appreciation figures (50–100% return on equity, 25–35% 2-year appreciation) sourced exclusively from broker marketing materials; no DLD transaction data or independent verification available
Palmiera secondary market price movement (~AED 8.4M to AED 13–14M) is broker-reported; not confirmed by DLD sales index
Total unit count discrepancy: Emaar official site states ~2,600 villas; third-party sources cite ~7,000 for full masterplan — likely reflects phased disclosure
Rental yield projections (5–7%) are broker estimates; no live rental transaction data available for this off-plan community
School driving times are approximate and sourced from area guides; not independently verified
Hospital driving times are partially unverified; Mediclinic Parkview exact distance not confirmed
Bayut source listing URLs provided but listing content not directly scraped; community data inferred from other sources
Mirage construction progress (19.22%) sourced from PropertyFinder as of November 2025; may be outdated
Palace Villas Ostra handover date varies across sources (Q3 2028 to Q3 2029); flagged as uncertain
Lavita unit count (43 mansions) sourced from single broker source (Alkira.ae); not confirmed by Emaar official communications
Public transport options are limited and developing; metro access requires ~15 min drive — community is effectively car-dependent
No confirmed on-site school or healthcare facility operational within the community boundary as of research date
Retail hub (~1.5M sq ft) is planned but not yet built or confirmed with opening timeline
